nuggets.idea_one_liner: Doom scrolling is a literal thief of time, stealing months of active life from your year while masquerading as simple relaxation.
nuggets.why_it_hits: Jarring mathematical reality check that turns a common habit into a terrifying loss of potential.

value_equation.dream_outcome: Reclaim 4.5 months of life per year

nuggets.proof_quote_redacted: That's how many days out of one year... full days... 136... divide that by 30... 4.55 [months]... literally wasting my life while I'm on my phone. Jesus. I didn't realize it was that bad.
proof_anchor_lines: Vast majority of people are literally giving away months of their life on an iPhone
The math of doom scrolling is a mirror of your wasted potential
nuggets.hook_angle_1_stakes: Your phone is charging you 4.5 months of rent per year.
nuggets.hook_angle_2_identity: The man who owns his thumb and his time.
nuggets.hook_angle_3_mechanism: The 'Phone-to-Month' conversion that will make you delete TikTok.
nuggets.enemy_frame_proof_redacted: You've been [curse] slacking your life away. What are you doing, bro? You have potential and you're just wasting it.
nuggets.first_step_seed: Check your Screen Time right now. Multiply daily average by 365, divide by 24. Look at that number of days and repent.
